2604.23354.title: Explainable AI in Speaker Recognition -- Making Latent Representations Understandable
2604.23354.paper_summary: Neural networks can be trained to learn task-relevant representations from data. Understanding how these networks make decisions falls within the Explainable AI (XAI) domain. This paper proposes to study an XAI topic: uncovering unknown organisational patterns in network representations, particularly those representations learned by the speaker recognition network that recognises the speaker identity of utterances. Past studies employed algorithms (e.g. t-distributed Stochastic Neighbour Embedding and K-means) to analyse and visualise how network representations form independent clusters, indicating the presence of flat clustering phenomena within the space defined by these representations. In contrast, this work applies two algorithms -- Single-Linkage Clustering (SLINK) and Hierarchical Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (HDBSCAN) -- to analyse how representations form clusters with hierarchical relationships rather than being independent, thereby demonstrating the existence of hierarchical clustering phenomena within the network representation space. To semantically understand the above hierarchical clustering phenomena, a new algorithm, termed Hierarchical Cluster-Class Matching (HCCM), is designed to perform one-to-one matching between predefined semantic classes and hierarchical representation clusters (i.e. those produced by SLINK or HDBSCAN). Some hierarchical clusters are successfully matched to individual semantic classes (e.g. male, UK), while others to conjunctions of semantic classes (e.g. male and UK, female and Ireland). A new metric, Liebig's score, is proposed to quantify the performance of each matching behaviour, allowing us to diagnose the factor that most strongly limits matching performance.
2604.23354.paper_summary_zh: 神經網絡可以被訓練以從數據中學習與任務相關的表示。理解這些網絡如何做出決策屬於可解釋人工智慧（XAI）領域。本文提議研究一個XAI主題：揭示網絡表示中的未知組織模式，特別是那些由識別說話者身份的說話者識別網絡學習的表示。過去的研究採用了算法（例如t-分佈隨機鄰居嵌入和K-means）來分析和可視化網絡表示如何形成獨立的聚類，這表明在這些表示所定義的空間中存在平坦聚類現象。相較之下，本研究應用了兩種算法——單鏈聚類（SLINK）和基於層次的噪音應用空間聚類（HDBSCAN）——來分析表示如何形成具有層次關係的聚類，而不是獨立的，從而展示了網絡表示空間中層次聚類現象的存在。為了語義上理解上述層次聚類現象，設計了一種新算法，稱為層次聚類類別匹配（HCCM），用於在預定義的語義類別和層次表示聚類（即由SLINK或HDBSCAN產生的聚類）之間進行一對一匹配。一些層次聚類成功地與個別語義類別（例如男性、英國）匹配，而其他則與語義類別的聯合（例如男性和英國、女性和愛爾蘭）匹配。提出了一種新指標，利比希分數，用於量化每種匹配行為的性能，讓我們能夠診斷出最強烈限制匹配性能的因素。
2604.23354.author: Yanze Xu et.al.
2604.23354.authors: Yanze Xu, Wenwu Wang, Mark D. Plumbley
